title: Normal-form Christoffel components
summary: Stores the ρ-derivative of the slice metric and its base Christoffel symbols, then defines the selected raised-index ambient Christoffel components used later.

mathematicalContent.heading: Selected normal-form components
mathematicalContent.body: Writing g′ᵢⱼ = ∂_ρgᵢⱼ and Γ(g_ρ) for the Levi-Civita symbols of the slice metric, the file records the ambient Christoffel components needed by later calculations. All triples not explicitly listed are assigned zero by the definition.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formalization boundary
mathematicalContent.body: ChristoffelComponents supplies g′ and the base symbols as independent fields. christoffelComp is a component table, so these displayed identities hold by definition; the file does not derive them from the metric formula or verify that baseGamma is the Levi-Civita connection of g_ρ.
mathematicalContent.formula: \widetilde\Gamma^K{}_{IJ}\ \text{is stipulated componentwise, not derived from }\ \frac12\widetilde g^{KL}(\partial_I\widetilde g_{JL}+\partial_J\widetilde g_{IL}-\partial_L\widetilde g_{IJ}).

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This extends the normal metric and inverse data by a formal ρ-derivative g′ᵢⱼ and formal base Christoffel symbols Γ(g_ρ)ᵏᵢⱼ.
correspondence.leanType: structure ChristoffelComponents (ι : Type w) extends NormalInverseMetricComponents ι

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This is the Kronecker delta restricted to base indices.
correspondence.leanType: def baseDelta [DecidableEq ι] (i j : ι) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Every diagonal base Kronecker component equals one.
correspondence.leanType: theorem baseDelta_self [DecidableEq ι] (i : ι) : baseDelta i i = 1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This function defines the selected raised-index Christoffel symbols in the 0, base, and ∞ directions and sets every unlisted triple to zero.
correspondence.leanType: def christoffelComp [DecidableEq ι] (C : ChristoffelComponents ι) : AmbientIndex ι → AmbientIndex ι → AmbientIndex ι → ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The upper scale component with two base inputs is −(t/2) times the ρ-derivative of the slice metric.
correspondence.leanType: theorem christoffel_zero_base_base [DecidableEq ι] (C : ChristoffelComponents ι) (i j : ι) : christoffelComp C AmbientIndex.zero (AmbientIndex.base i) (AmbientIndex.base j) = -(1 / 2 : ℝ) * C.t * C.dRhoG i j

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: With an upper base index, one scale input and one base input give t⁻¹ times the Kronecker delta.
correspondence.leanType: theorem christoffel_base_zero_base [DecidableEq ι] (C : ChristoffelComponents ι) (k j : ι) : christoffelComp C (AmbientIndex.base k) AmbientIndex.zero (AmbientIndex.base j) = C.t⁻¹ * baseDelta j k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Interchanging the two lower inputs gives the same component.
correspondence.leanType: theorem christoffel_base_base_zero [DecidableEq ι] (C : ChristoffelComponents ι) (k j : ι) : christoffelComp C (AmbientIndex.base k) (AmbientIndex.base j) AmbientIndex.zero = C.t⁻¹ * baseDelta j k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The upper transverse component with two base inputs combines the slice metric and its ρ-derivative.
correspondence.leanType: theorem christoffel_inf_base_base [DecidableEq ι] (C : ChristoffelComponents ι) (i j : ι) : christoffelComp C AmbientIndex.inf (AmbientIndex.base i) (AmbientIndex.base j) = -C.g i j + C.rho * C.dRhoG i j

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The all-base ambient component is identified with the supplied Christoffel symbol of the slice metric g_ρ.
correspondence.leanType: theorem christoffel_base_base_base [DecidableEq ι] (C : ChristoffelComponents ι) (k i j : ι) : christoffelComp C (AmbientIndex.base k) (AmbientIndex.base i) (AmbientIndex.base j) = C.baseGamma k i j

references.locator: Chapter 3, Levi-Civita connection in normal form
references.note: Source for the displayed normal-form Christoffel components.

limitations: The Christoffel formulas are encoded by definition from supplied fields. No differentiation of the ambient metric, compatibility, torsion-freeness, or derivation from the Levi-Civita formula is formalized in this file.
